name: registry
context: Push, pull, and manage container images in Docker registries (Docker Hub, GHCR, ECR)
info: Docker registries store and distribute container images. Docker Hub is the default public registry. GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) hosts images alongside source code. AWS ECR provides private registries with IAM integration. This skill covers authenticating with registries via `docker login`, tagging images with the full registry path, pushing and pulling images, managing image tags and digests, and configuring private registries. Multi-platform images can be pushed using `docker buildx` with manifest lists. Image signing and vulnerability scanning add security layers before deployment.
body:
# Docker Registry

A Docker registry stores and distributes container images. After building an image (see [[docker/images/build]]), push it to a registry so it can be pulled by other machines, CI/CD pipelines, or orchestrators.

## Login

Authenticate before pushing to any registry:

```bash
# Docker Hub
docker login -u $DOCKER_USERNAME -p $DOCKER_PASSWORD

# GitHub Container Registry (GHCR)
echo $GITHUB_TOKEN | docker login ghcr.io -u $GITHUB_USERNAME --password-stdin

# AWS ECR
aws ecr get-login-password --region us-east-1 | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
```

Always use `--password-stdin` in scripts to avoid exposing credentials in process lists.

## Push and Pull

Images must be tagged with the full registry path before pushing:

```bash
# Tag for Docker Hub
docker tag myapp:latest myusername/myapp:v1.0.0
docker push myusername/myapp:v1.0.0

# Tag for GHCR
docker tag myapp:latest ghcr.io/myorg/myapp:v1.0.0
docker push ghcr.io/myorg/myapp:v1.0.0

# Tag for ECR
docker tag myapp:latest $AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/myapp:v1.0.0
docker push $AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/myapp:v1.0.0

# Pull an image
docker pull nginx:alpine
docker pull ghcr.io/myorg/myapp:v1.0.0
```

## Tagging Strategies

Consistent tagging enables reliable deployments:

| Tag Pattern | Use Case |
|-------------|----------|
| `v1.0.0` | Semantic versioned releases |
| `latest` | Most recent build (mutable, use with caution) |
| `sha-abc1234` | Git commit SHA for traceability |
| `main`, `develop` | Branch-based tags for CI/CD |
| `20260410` | Date-based tags for nightly builds |

Always push both a version tag and `latest` for release builds. Use immutable tags (semver, SHA) for production.

## Private Registries

| Registry | URL Pattern | Auth Method |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Docker Hub | `docker.io/username/image` | Username + password/token |
| GHCR | `ghcr.io/org/image` | GitHub PAT or GITHUB_TOKEN |
| AWS ECR | `account.dkr.ecr.region.amazonaws.com/image` | AWS CLI + IAM |
| GCP Artifact Registry | `region-docker.pkg.dev/project/repo/image` | `gcloud auth configure-docker` |
| Self-hosted | `registry.example.com/image` | Username + password, TLS certs |

For CI/CD, store registry credentials as secrets (GitHub Actions secrets, AWS Secrets Manager). Never hardcode credentials in Dockerfiles or Compose files.

## Image Digests

Every pushed image has an immutable SHA256 digest:

```bash
# Get digest after push
docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' myapp:v1.0.0

# Pull by digest (immutable)
docker pull myregistry/myapp@sha256:abc123...
```

Use digests in production for guaranteed reproducibility. Tags are mutable and can be overwritten.

sections.title: Registry authentication
sections.body: Use `docker login` with `--password-stdin` for secure authentication. Docker Hub uses username/password or access tokens. GHCR uses GitHub PATs or the `GITHUB_TOKEN` in CI. AWS ECR uses `aws ecr get-login-password` piped to `docker login`. Store credentials securely — never in Dockerfiles, Compose files, or source control.

sections.title: Pushing and pulling images
sections.body: Tag images with the full registry path before pushing: `docker tag local:tag registry/repo:tag`. Push with `docker push`. Pull with `docker pull`. Images are pulled automatically by [[docker/compose]] when using `image:` in service definitions. For [[docker/images/build]], use `docker buildx build --push` to build and push in one step.

sections.title: Tagging strategies
sections.body: Use semantic versioning (`v1.0.0`) for releases. Git commit SHAs (`sha-abc1234`) for traceability. Branch names (`main`, `develop`) for CI/CD. Date stamps for nightly builds. Always push both a versioned tag and `latest` for releases. Prefer immutable tags (semver, digest) in production — `latest` is mutable and unreliable for reproducibility.

sections.title: Private registries
sections.body: Docker Hub, GHCR, AWS ECR, GCP Artifact Registry, and self-hosted registries all support private images. Configure authentication per registry. In CI/CD, use platform-native secrets (GitHub Actions secrets, AWS IAM roles). Self-hosted registries require TLS certificates and may need a credentials store. See [[docker/compose]] for using private images in Compose files.
